creator: aviator45003@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Steven Hartland from comment #1)

It is a regular thing - it occurs every boot from installed media in UEFI mode

The machine is stable running FreeBSD in BIOS mode right now.

The only way to get it to work was installing BIOS mode, booting into the install, rebooting into the UEFI installer, and installing in UEFI mode. Only then would it successfully boot. Not a ranom failure, that printout you saw could happen any time I booted with no differences.

I have not tried a memory test.

I can try burning HEAD to a disk and testing it if that is the best solution.

http://imgur.com/a/uS6g6 is what it looks like when I try entering things at the prompt, on a different attempt to boot an installed UEFI FreeBSD.
